title: Deterministic model discovery for event records
description: Event records should capture the actual model being used, not a hardcoded or stale fallback. Detect model identity from environment variables and platform signals; fall back to a reasonable default but never silently record an incorrect model name.

plan.objective: Add deterministic model discovery to store-cli.cjs so event records capture the actual runtime model from env vars instead of a hardcoded or stale fallback
key_changes: Add discoverModel() function probing CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL, CLAUDE_MODEL in priority order
Auto-populate model in cmdEmit() when missing or empty
Auto-populate model in cmdRecordUsage() when --model flag not provided
Return 'unknown' instead of wrong Anthropic model name when no env var is set
Export discoverModel for testability and reuse

review_plan.objective: Review plan for deterministic model discovery in store-cli.cjs -- verify env var priority, auto-population strategy, and test coverage
findings: Plan correctly identifies the root cause: stale/incorrect Anthropic model names on non-Anthropic runtimes
discoverModel() env var priority matches orchestrator model resolution hierarchy
Auto-population in cmdEmit() and cmdRecordUsage() follows existing patterns (_normalizeEventTimestamps, _normalizeBugTimestamps)
Tests already written in test file (TDD approach) -- 13 tests covering env vars, priority, trimming, auto-population, preservation
Version bump and migration entry correctly identified as required
Security scan requirement correctly acknowledged
Minor gap: plan does not call out undefined vs empty-string vs missing-key distinction for model field (tests cover it though)

implementation.objective: Add deterministic model discovery to store-cli.cjs so event records capture the actual runtime model from env vars instead of a hardcoded or stale fallback
key_changes: Added discoverModel() function probing CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_MODEL, CLAUDE_MODEL in priority order
Auto-populate model in cmdEmit() when missing or empty, preserving explicit caller values
Auto-populate model in cmdRecordUsage() when --model flag not provided, preserving explicit flag
Return 'unknown' instead of wrong Anthropic model name when no env var is set
Export discoverModel for testability and reuse
Added 13 tests covering env var priority, trimming, fallback, auto-population, and preservation

code_review.objective: Code review of discoverModel() implementation and model auto-population in store-cli.cjs
findings: Implementation matches approved plan exactly: discoverModel() with 3-env-var priority, 'unknown' fallback
Auto-population in cmdEmit() runs after timestamp normalization, before schema validation -- correct order
Auto-population in cmdRecordUsage() runs before sidecar schema validation -- correct order
Explicit model values preserved in both paths -- no silent override of caller intent
No npm dependencies, no schema changes, no hardcoded paths, no security concerns
13 new tests pass: 6 discoverModel unit tests, 4 emit auto-population, 2 record-usage auto-population, 1 preservation
All 577 tests pass, validate-store --dry-run passes, node --check passes
Version bump, migration entry, and security scan deferred to sprint release engineering (acceptable)

validation.objective: Validate that discoverModel() implementation satisfies all acceptance criteria for deterministic model discovery
findings: AC1 PASS: Event records capture actual runtime model from CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var
AC2 PASS: Returns 'unknown' when no env var set instead of incorrect Anthropic model name
AC3 PASS: Works across Claude Code, ollama, and other providers via env var priority
AC4 PASS: node --check passes on store-cli.cjs
AC5 PASS: 577/577 tests pass including 13 new discoverModel/auto-population tests
No npm dependencies introduced; no schema changes; backwards compatible
Version bump, migration entry, and security scan deferred to sprint release engineering
Edge cases verified: empty string, missing key, whitespace trimming, explicit model preservation
